Belgian footballer (born 2004)

Sekou Diawara

**Sekou Diawara** (born 8 February 2004) is a Belgian professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays as a [striker](/wiki/Striker_(association_football) "Striker (association football)") for [Swiss Promotion League](/wiki/Promotion_League "Promotion League") side [Zürich U21](/wiki/FC_Z%C3%BCrich "FC Zürich").

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sekou_Diawara&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

A youth product of [Gent](/wiki/K.A.A._Gent "K.A.A. Gent"), Diawara moved to [Genk](/wiki/K.R.C._Genk "K.R.C. Genk")'s youth academy in 2018.[[2]](#cite_note-2) On 7 August 2020 he signed his first professional contract to Gent until 2023, and was later promoted to Jong Genk in 2022.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He had a falling out with Genk after being suspended for posing with his brother Mamady celebrating the latters' move to [Club Brugge](/wiki/Club_Brugge_KV "Club Brugge KV").[[4]](#cite_note-4) On 25 January 2023, he moved to the Italian club [Udinese](/wiki/Udinese_Calcio "Udinese Calcio") on a contract until 2027.[[5]](#cite_note-5) He made his senior debut with Udinese in a 2–1 [Coppa Italia](/wiki/Coppa_Italia "Coppa Italia") loss in extra time to [Cagliari](/wiki/Cagliari_Calcio "Cagliari Calcio") on 12 December 2023.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

On 25 January 2024, Diawara moved on loan to [Beerschot](/wiki/K_Beerschot_VA "K Beerschot VA") until the end of the season.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

On 6 September 2024, Diawara joined [Challenger Pro League](/wiki/Challenger_Pro_League "Challenger Pro League") club [Francs Borains](/wiki/Francs_Borains "Francs Borains").[[8]](#cite_note-8) On 9 January 2025, Francs Borains announced Diawara's return to Udinese.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

On 7 November 2025, Diawara signed a three-year contract with [Zürich](/wiki/FC_Z%C3%BCrich "FC Zürich") in Switzerland and was assigned to their Under-21 squad that plays in the third-tier [Promotion League](/wiki/Promotion_League "Promotion League").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sekou_Diawara&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Born in Belgium, Diawara is of Guinean descent.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He is a youth international for Belgium, having played for the [Belgium 20s](/wiki/Belgium_national_under-20_football_team "Belgium national under-20 football team") in September 2023.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
